title: constant propagation cannot be deal with anti join
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```
CREATE TABLE `t2` (
  `k` int(11) DEFAULT NULL,
  `b` int(11) DEFAULT NULL
);
CREATE TABLE `t3` (
  `k` int(11) DEFAULT NULL,
  `b` int(11) DEFAULT NULL
) ENGINE=InnoDB DEFAULT CHARSET=utf8mb4 COLLATE=utf8mb4_bin
PARTITION BY HASH (`k`) PARTITIONS 6;

explain select * from t2 where not exists (select 1 from t3 where t3.k = t2.k and t3.b = t2.b) and t2.k = 1;

```
<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

tested with PgSQL

```
weizhenwang@/tmp:postgres> explain EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM t2
 WHERE NOT EXISTS (
     SELECT 1 FROM t3
     WHERE t3.k = t2.k AND t3.b = t2.b
 )
 AND t2.k = 1;
weizhenwang@/tmp:postgres> EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM t2
 WHERE NOT EXISTS (
     SELECT 1 FROM t3
     WHERE t3.k = t2.k AND t3.b = t2.b
 )
 AND t2.k = 1;
+----------------------------------------------------------------+
| QUERY PLAN                                                     |
|----------------------------------------------------------------|
| Hash Anti Join  (cost=38.41..76.82 rows=10 width=8)            |
|   Hash Cond: ((t2.k = t3.k) AND (t2.b = t3.b))                 |
|   ->  Seq Scan on t2  (cost=0.00..38.25 rows=11 width=8)       |
|         Filter: (k = 1)                                        |
|   ->  Hash  (cost=38.25..38.25 rows=11 width=8)                |
|         ->  Seq Scan on t3  (cost=0.00..38.25 rows=11 width=8) |
|               Filter: (k = 1)                                  |
+----------------------------------------------------------------+
EXPLAIN 7
Time: 0.013s
```

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

```
+-----------------------------+---------+-----------+---------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| id                          | estRows | task      | access object | operator info                                                             |
+-----------------------------+---------+-----------+---------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------+
| HashJoin_9                  | 0.00    | root      |               | anti semi join, equal:[eq(test.t2.k, test.t3.k) eq(test.t2.b, test.t3.b)] |
| ├─TableReader_14(Build)     | 1.00    | root      | partition:all | data:TableFullScan_13                                                     |
| │ └─TableFullScan_13        | 1.00    | cop[tikv] | table:t3      | keep order:false                                                          |
| └─TableReader_12(Probe)     | 0.00    | root      |               | data:Selection_11                                                         |
|   └─Selection_11            | 0.00    | cop[tikv] |               | eq(test.t2.k, 1)                                                          |
|     └─TableFullScan_10      | 3.00    | cop[tikv] | table:t2      | keep order:false                                                          |
+-----------------------------+---------+-----------+---------------+---------------------------------------------------------------------------+
6 rows in set (0.00 sec)
```

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t4 (id INTEGER NOT NULL, value TEXT DEFAULT NULL);
CREATE TABLE t5 (id INTEGER NOT NULL, value INTEGER DEFAULT NULL, UNIQUE(value));
INSERT INTO t4 (id, value) VALUES (1, 'test'), (2, NULL), (3, 'example');
INSERT INTO t5 (id, value) VALUES (1, 10), (2, 20), (3, NULL);
EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN SELECT * FROM t4 WHERE NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM t5 WHERE t5.value = t4.value) AND t4.id = 1;
key_bug_features: constant propagation with anti join in SQL queries
